title: What is the CSS nesting syntax?
description: How does native CSS nesting work?
options: Native CSS nesting allows writing selectors inside other selectors: .parent { .child { color: red; } &:hover { bg: blue; } }. Uses & for parent reference. Reduces repetition similar to Sass/LESS but built into browsers
correct_answer: Native CSS nesting allows writing selectors inside other selectors: .parent { .child { color: red; } &:hover { bg: blue; } }. Uses & for parent reference. Reduces repetition similar to Sass/LESS but built into browsers

title: What is the difference between loading=lazy and Intersection Observer?
description: Compare native lazy loading and IO approaches.
options: loading='lazy' is a simple HTML attribute for images/iframes with browser-managed thresholds; IntersectionObserver gives full control over margins, thresholds, and actions. Use loading=lazy for simple cases; IO for custom behavior
correct_answer: loading='lazy' is a simple HTML attribute for images/iframes with browser-managed thresholds; IntersectionObserver gives full control over margins, thresholds, and actions. Use loading=lazy for simple cases; IO for custom behavior

title: What is the View Transitions API?
description: How does the View Transitions API work?
options: document.startViewTransition() captures old and new DOM states, creating CSS animations between them. Uses ::view-transition pseudo-elements for styling. Enables page-level transitions without frameworks; works with MPA in Chrome 126+
correct_answer: document.startViewTransition() captures old and new DOM states, creating CSS animations between them. Uses ::view-transition pseudo-elements for styling. Enables page-level transitions without frameworks; works with MPA in Chrome 126+

title: What is the difference between visibility:hidden and opacity:0?
options: display:none removes from layout and accessibility tree. visibility:hidden reserves space, hidden from screen but accessible. opacity:0 reserves space, can still receive events, accessible. Each has different interaction with transitions
correct_answer: display:none removes from layout and accessibility tree. visibility:hidden reserves space, hidden from screen but accessible. opacity:0 reserves space, can still receive events, accessible. Each has different interaction with transitions

title: What is the CSS color-mix() function?
options: color-mix(in srgb, red 30%, blue) blends two colors in a given color space. Supports srgb, oklch, hsl, etc. Enables dynamic color generation, tints, shades without preprocessors. Can use with custom properties for theme systems
correct_answer: color-mix(in srgb, red 30%, blue) blends two colors in a given color space. Supports srgb, oklch, hsl, etc. Enables dynamic color generation, tints, shades without preprocessors. Can use with custom properties for theme systems

title: What is the CSS :where() pseudo-class?
options: :where() and :is() both accept selector lists; the key difference is :where() always has 0 specificity while :is() takes the highest specificity of its arguments. Use :where() for easily-overridable base styles
correct_answer: :where() and :is() both accept selector lists; the key difference is :where() always has 0 specificity while :is() takes the highest specificity of its arguments. Use :where() for easily-overridable base styles

title: What is the difference between min-content, max-content, and fit-content?
options: min-content: shrinks to narrowest without overflow (longest word). max-content: expands to fit all content on one line. fit-content(max): acts like min(max-content, max(min-content, max)). Used in grid/flex sizing for content-aware layouts
correct_answer: min-content: shrinks to narrowest without overflow (longest word). max-content: expands to fit all content on one line. fit-content(max): acts like min(max-content, max(min-content, max)). Used in grid/flex sizing for content-aware layouts

title: What is the anchor positioning API in CSS?
description: How does CSS anchor positioning work?
options: Allows positioning elements relative to other elements (anchors) without JS. anchor-name defines an anchor; position-anchor references it; anchor() function calculates positions. Replaces JS positioning for tooltips, popovers, dropdowns
correct_answer: Allows positioning elements relative to other elements (anchors) without JS. anchor-name defines an anchor; position-anchor references it; anchor() function calculates positions. Replaces JS positioning for tooltips, popovers, dropdowns
